howItWorks.intro: This cholesterol ratio calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Health metrics are general guidelines. Consult your healthcare provider for personalized medical advice.

faq.question: What is a good Total/HDL ratio?
faq.answer: Below 3.5 is ideal. 3.5-5.0 is acceptable. Above 5.0 indicates elevated cardiovascular risk. This ratio is often more predictive than total cholesterol alone.

faq.question: Why does the Trig/HDL ratio matter?
faq.answer: Trig/HDL under 2.0 suggests healthy insulin sensitivity. Over 3.0 may indicate insulin resistance and metabolic syndrome, even with otherwise normal cholesterol.

faq.question: How do I improve my ratios?
faq.answer: Raise HDL: exercise, omega-3s, moderate alcohol. Lower LDL: reduce saturated fat, eat fiber, consider statins. Lower triglycerides: reduce sugar, alcohol, and refined carbs.

faq.question: How often should I test?
faq.answer: Every 4-6 years for adults with normal levels. Annually if levels are abnormal or you have risk factors. More often if on cholesterol medication.
